checks.a_analysis: The output JSON contains only information present in the source PDF. The headings, list items, and table content correspond directly to the text and structure found in the original document. No invented introductions, conclusions, or sections were found.

There are no literal phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs in the source document. The 'Phone' columns in the attendance tables refer to the presence status (attended via phone), which is correctly transcribed as 'X'. All dates and times match the source.
All sections of the 3-page document are accounted for in the JSON output, including the header, meeting attendees, staff list, agenda items 1-11, and the adjournment/next meeting information.

The output contains all rows from the source tables. The 'Committee Members' and 'Guests' tables from page 1 were merged into a single structural table in the JSON, which preserves all 12 individuals (9 members, 3 guests). The Staff table (11 rows) and the two vote tables (5 rows each) are also fully represented.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: No significant text omissions were identified. Long sections such as the Legislative Review and Technology Update were converted in full, including content spanning page breaks.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text content matches the source document with high fidelity. Minor structural changes (such as merging side-by-side tables or using nested lists for sub-points) are consistent with accessibility-optimized conversion and do not alter the meaning of the information.

checks.a_analysis: The output JSON represents all text blocks from the source document exactly once. There are no duplicated paragraphs, headings, or list items. The meeting title and subtitles are correctly distributed into metadata and section headers without repetition.

The heading hierarchy is logically constructed and follows standard accessibility practices. The main title of the document is used as the section title, followed by H2 and H3 for sub-headers (Approved Minutes, Date). The 'Agenda' section is correctly marked with an H2. No heading levels are skipped.

The conversion faithfully represents all tables. Notably, the two side-by-side tables on page 1 (Committee Members and Guests) have been intelligently merged into a single 6-column table, which correctly preserves the horizontal alignment of the data. The Staff attendance table and the vote tables on page 2 are also correctly structured with appropriate headers and data rows.

The reading order in the output JSON matches the logical flow of the source document perfectly. It follows the header information, attendee tables, and then the agenda items in numerical order. The vote tables are placed immediately after the agenda items they relate to (items 4 and 5), matching the source document's layout.

The agenda list (1-11) is correctly identified and converted. Although the list is technically split into three list elements to accommodate interleaved tables, the sequential flow and item content are preserved. The sub-list under item 10 (a, b, c) is correctly nested within the parent list item. Bullet/numbering meaning is retained in the text content.
There are no links in the source document, and no links have been inappropriately injected into the output JSON. All content is represented as plain text or structured elements as expected.

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains a single continuous numbered list for the 'Agenda' section, items 1 through 11. In the HTML output, this list is visually broken into three separate numbered lists. The first list contains items 1-4. Following the first table, a new list begins, incorrectly restarting at '1.' for the item that should be 5. Following the second table, another new list begins, again restarting at '1.' for the item that should be 6, and continuing to 6. This fragmentation significantly disrupts the visual organization and the sequential flow intended in the source document.
checks.b_evidence_source: Numbered agenda items 1 through 11 across pages 1, 2, and 3.
checks.b_evidence_output: Three separate numbered lists: 1-4, then 1, then 1-6.

checks.a_analysis: All tables from the source PDF are rendered clearly in the HTML. The 'Committee Members' and 'Guests' tables from page 1 of the PDF are combined into a single side-by-side table in the HTML, which maintains the visual relationship of the data effectively. Headers are distinct, and cell alignment is correct. The voting tables in items 4 and 5 are also properly rendered with visible borders and aligned columns.

The NC DIT 911 Board logo from the first page of the PDF is present in the HTML output. The background watermark ('Approved') is omitted in the HTML, which is considered an intentional accessibility improvement and not a defect.

There is no duplication of body content such as paragraphs or tables. While section headings appear in both the sidebar 'Contents' navigation and the main body, this is an expected accessibility feature and does not constitute a content duplication defect.
